reason: The duet harness booted both standalone runtimes, registered both eliza-1-0_6b bundles, armed both engines' voice lifecycles, and drove engine.load() — at which point the locally-installed linux-x64-cpu-fused llama-server failed with 'error while loading shared libraries: libmtmd.so.0: cannot open shared object file'. That is a broken local fused build (a WS-2/WS-3 build-matrix item), not a harness bug. The real-output CPU baseline run is blocked until a working fused build is present. No numbers are fabricated.
notes: Needs a working fused llama-server build to run; this artifact is the honest placeholder so eliza1_gates_collect.mjs records the duet gates as needs-data, not a fabricated pass. The static structured-decode token-savings aggregate over the synthetic action set is ~28% forced (WS-4's guided_decode_token_bench.mjs); the per-run number is needs-run. The GGUF-ASR emotion-label surface is unconfirmed (WS-4 probes eliza-1-asr-mmproj.gguf) so emotionFidelity.accuracy is null with perceiver: fallback-classifier (unavailable).
